title: Ponera graminifolia
taxonomy: Ponera graminifolia is a species of ant in the family Formicidae. Ponera graminifolia is a member of the genus Ponera, which is a group of ants characterized by their distinctive elongated bodies. Ponera graminifolia ants are generally small and have a brownish color. They are part of the subfamily Ponerinae, which includes ants known for their unique social behavior and nesting habits. Ponera graminifolia is also classified as a part of the order Hymenoptera, which encompasses a wide range of insects including bees, wasps, and sawflies.
